_note: Visual/interactive clue types the tool cannot text-solve. Each maps to the wiki guide so the user gets a clickable link to where the answer/solver lives. Hand-authored static reference.

map.blurb: Map clues are a hand-drawn map of the dig site. Match yours against the maps in the guide, then dig at the matching spot:

puzzle box.blurb: A puzzle box is a sliding-tile picture puzzle solved in-client. See the step-by-step solutions:

light box.blurb: A light box is a logic puzzle of lights to toggle on. See the solver:

hot cold.blurb: Hot/Cold clues use the Strange Device, which runs hotter the closer you are. Narrow the dig spot down with the location guide:

compass.blurb: this one's genuinely not worth a lookup — the in-game compass points an arrow straight at the dig spot. Follow the arrow until it spins, then dig. It's one of the easiest clue steps.

scan.blurb: A scan clue is solved with the in-game scanner that pulses stronger near the spot. See the per-area scan guide:

celtic knot.blurb: A Celtic knot is an in-client logic puzzle of rotating rings. See the solver:

lockbox.blurb: A lockbox is an in-client sliding puzzle. See the solver:
